volumeTitle: The Woman of Andros. The Self-Tormentor. The Eunuch
label: The Self-Tormentor (Section 5.1.894d - Section 5.1.947b)

sections.label: Section 5.1.894d
sections.translation: Nor yet your son himself ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.894e
sections.translation: Not in the slightest , Chremes .

sections.label: Section 5.1.895
sections.original: Magis unum etiam instare ut hodie conficerentur nuptiae .
sections.translation: He was only the more pressing on this one point , that the match might be concluded to-day .

sections.label: Section 5.1.896
sections.original: Mira narras . Quid Syrus meus ? ne is quidem quicquam ?
sections.translation: You say what ’ s surprising . What did my servant Syrus do ? Didn ’ t even he say any thing ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.896b
sections.translation: Nothing at all .

sections.label: Section 5.1.897
sections.translation: For what reason , I don ’ t know .

sections.label: Section 5.1.897b
sections.original: Equidem miror , qui alia tam plane scias .
sections.translation: For my part , I wonder at that , when you know other things so well . But this same Syrus has moulded your son , [*] too , to such perfection , that there could not be even the slightest suspicion that she is Clinia ’ s mistress !

sections.label: Section 5.1.900
sections.translation: What do you say ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.900b
sections.original: Mitto iam osculari atque amplexari : id nihil puto .
sections.translation: Not to mention , then , their kissing and embracing ; that I count nothing .

sections.label: Section 5.1.901
sections.translation: What more could be done to carry on the cheat ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.901b
sections.translation: Pshaw !

sections.label: Section 5.1.901c
sections.translation: What do you mean ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.901d
sections.translation: Only listen . In the inner part of my house there is a certain room at the back ; into this a bed was brought , and was made up with bed-clothes .

sections.label: Section 5.1.904
sections.translation: What took place after this ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.904b
sections.translation: No sooner said than done , thither went Clitipho .

sections.label: Section 5.1.905
sections.translation: Alone ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.905b
sections.translation: Alone .

sections.label: Section 5.1.905c
sections.translation: I ’ m alarmed .

sections.label: Section 5.1.905d
sections.translation: Bacchis followed directly .

sections.label: Section 5.1.906
sections.translation: Alone ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.906b
sections.translation: Alone .

sections.label: Section 5.1.906c
sections.translation: I ’ m undone !

sections.label: Section 5.1.906d
sections.translation: When they had gone into the room , they shut the door .

sections.label: Section 5.1.906e
sections.translation: Well — did Clinia see all this going on ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.907b
sections.translation: How shouldn ’ t he ? He was with me .

sections.label: Section 5.1.908
sections.original: Fili est amica Bacchis , Menedeme . Occidi .
sections.translation: Bacchis is my son ’ s mistress , Menedemus I ’ m undone .

sections.label: Section 5.1.909
sections.translation: Why so ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.909b
sections.original: Decem dierum vix mihi est familia .
sections.translation: I have hardly substance to suffice for ten days . [*]

sections.label: Section 5.1.910
sections.original: Quid ? istuc times quod ille operam amico dat suo ?
sections.translation: What ! are you alarmed at it , because he is paying attention to his friend ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.911
sections.translation: His she-friend rather . [*]

sections.label: Section 5.1.911b
sections.translation: If he really is paying it .

sections.label: Section 5.1.911c
sections.translation: Is it a matter of doubt to you ? Do you suppose that there is any person of so accommodating and tame a spirit as to suffer his own mistress , himself looking on , to —

sections.label: Section 5.1.914
sections.original: Quidni ? quo verba facilius dentur mihi .
sections.translation: Why not ? That I may be imposed upon the more easily .

sections.label: Section 5.1.915
sections.original: Derides merito . Mihi nunc ego succenseo .
sections.translation: Do you laugh at me ? You have good reason . How angry I now am with myself ! How many things gave proof , whereby , had I not been a stone , I might have been fully sensible of this ? What was it I saw ? Alas ! wretch that I am ! But assuredly they shall not escape my vengeance if I live ; for this instant —

sections.label: Section 5.1.919b
sections.original: Non tu te cohibes ? non te respicis ?
sections.translation: Can you not contain yourself ? Have you no respect for yourself ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.920
sections.original: Non tibi ego exempli satis sum ?
sections.translation: Am I not a sufficient example to you ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.920b
sections.translation: For very anger , Menedemus , I am not myself .

sections.label: Section 5.1.921b
sections.translation: For you to talk in that manner ! Is it not a shame for you to be giving advice to others , to show wisdom abroad and yet be able to do nothing for yourself ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.924
sections.translation: What shall I do ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.924b
sections.original: Id quod me fecisse aiebas parum .
sections.translation: That which you said I failed to do :

sections.label: Section 5.1.925
sections.original: Fac te patrem esse sentiat ; fac ut audeat
sections.translation: make him sensible that you are his father ; make him venture to intrust every thing to you , to seek and to ask of you ; so that he may look for no other resources and forsake you . [*]

sections.label: Section 5.1.928
sections.original: Immo abeat multo malo quovis gentium ,
sections.translation: Nay , I had much rather he would go any where in the world , than by his debaucheries here reduce his father to beggary !

sections.label: Section 5.1.930
sections.original: Nam , si illi pergo suppeditare sumtibus ,
sections.translation: For if I go on supplying his extravagance , Menedemus , in that case my circumstances will undoubtedly be soon reduced to the level of your rake .

sections.label: Section 5.1.932
sections.original: Quot incommoditates in hac re accipies , nisi caves ?
sections.translation: What evils you will bring upon yourself in this affair , if you don ’ t act with caution ! You ’ ll show yourself severe , and still pardon him at last ; that too with an ill grace .

sections.label: Section 5.1.934b
sections.translation: Ah ! you don ’ t know how vexed I am .

sections.label: Section 5.1.934c
sections.translation: Just as you please .

sections.label: Section 5.1.935
sections.original: Quid hoc quod volo , ut illa nubat nostro ? nisi quid est
sections.translation: What about that which I desire — that she may be married to my son ? Unless there is any other step that you would prefer .

sections.label: Section 5.1.936b
sections.original: Immo et gener et affines placent .
sections.translation: On the contrary , both the son-in-law and the connection are to my taste .

sections.label: Section 5.1.937
sections.original: Quid dotis dicam te dixisse filio ?
sections.translation: What portion shall I say that you have named for your daughter ? Why are you silent ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.938b
sections.translation: Portion ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.938c
sections.translation: I say so .

sections.label: Section 5.1.938d
sections.translation: Alas !

sections.label: Section 5.1.938e
sections.translation: Chremes , don ’ t be at all afraid to speak , if it is but a small one . The portion is no consideration at all with us .

sections.label: Section 5.1.940
sections.original: Duo talenta pro re nostra ego esse decrevi satis .
sections.translation: I did think that two talents were sufficient , according to my means . But if you wish me to be saved , and my estate and my son , you must say to this effect , that I have settled all my property on her as her portion .

sections.label: Section 5.1.942b
sections.translation: What scheme are you upon ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.943
sections.original: Id mirari te simulato , et illum hoc rogitato simul ,
sections.translation: Pretend that you wonder at this , and at the same time ask him the reason why I do so .

sections.label: Section 5.1.944b
sections.original: Quin ego vero quamobrem id facias nescio .
sections.translation: Why , really , I can ’ t conceive the reason for your doing so .

sections.label: Section 5.1.945
sections.original: Egone ? ut eius animum , qui nunc luxuria et lascivia
sections.translation: Why do I do so ? To check his feelings , which are now hurried away by luxury and wantonness , and to bring him down so as not to know which way to turn himself .

sections.label: Section 5.1.947
sections.translation: What is your design ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.947b
sections.original: Mitte ; sine me in hac re gerere mihi morem .
sections.translation: Let me alone , and give me leave to have my own way in this matter .
